Scenario.title: FunnyDream Campaign Targets Water Treatment Facilities via Spearphishing
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Intermediate A sophisticated attack chain targeting critical infrastructure involves spearphishing, credential exploitation, and SCADA manipulation to disrupt water treatment operations.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 511: Infiltration of Software Development Environment
stage.description: Attackers sent targeted emails containing malicious documents to employees, exploiting human trust to install malware and steal credentials.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 108: Command Line Execution through SQL Injection
stage.description: Weaponized PowerShell scripts were executed remotely using stolen credentials to deploy payloads and establish control.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: Hidden scheduled tasks were created to maintain access across system reboots using compromised account privileges.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Stolen credentials were leveraged to escalate privileges and gain administrative access to SCADA systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 636: Hiding Malicious Data or Code within Files
stage.description: Payloads and communications were encrypted and obfuscated to evade detection by legacy security tools.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522: Malicious Hardware Component Replacement
stage.description: Attackers altered SCADA system parameters to disrupt water treatment processes, risking public health and operational integrity.

Scenario.summary: The FunnyDream campaign exploited human and technical vulnerabilities in water treatment facilities through spearphishing, privilege escalation, and SCADA manipulation to cause critical infrastructure disruptions.
